steps.title: Use the zero product rule
steps.content:
A product equals $0$ when at least one factor equals $0$. For
$$3x(x-4)(x+6)=0$$
the factors to check are $3x$, $x-4$, and $x+6$.

steps.title: Find a listed solution
steps.content:
Set each variable factor equal to $0$.
$$x=0$$
$$x-4=0,\ x=4$$
$$x+6=0,\ x=-6$$
Only one of these appears among the choices. The answer is B.
